behavior: Forages in shallow water by standing, stalking, running, or stirring the bottom with its feet, then striking at small aquatic prey. Often occurs singly or in loose groups and nests or roosts communally.
field_marks: Small, slender heron with a long neck and compact body
Usually all-white plumage; occasional dark morph is bluish slate-gray
Long blackish legs with contrasting yellow feet
Long folded wings taper toward a short tail
habitat: Open fresh, brackish, and coastal wetlands, including lakeshores, rivers, marshes, estuaries, lagoons, mudflats, mangroves, flooded fields, and other shallow waters rich in small fish.
